title: fixtures_emission
description: Output contract for the fixtures/ subpackage. The model emits JSON conforming to this schema; the deterministic writer at .claude/melleafy/writers/fixtures_writer.py renders the per-fixture .py files plus fixtures/__init__.py. The model never writes Python source for fixtures directly — this prevents drift shapes (pytest-style tests, INPUT-only modules, etc.) that would violate the loader contract enforced by file_utils.load_fixtures.

fixtures.description: Ordered list of test fixtures. Length must satisfy R16 (5-8 fixtures). Order is preserved in fixtures/__init__.py's ALL_FIXTURES.

coverage_doc.description: Optional. Multi-line text rendered as the module docstring at the top of fixtures/__init__.py. Should document which fixtures exercise which C-categories (R16's coverage threshold).

id.description: snake_case identifier; must be unique within fixtures[]; used as the .py filename stem (fixtures/<id>.py), the factory function name (make_<id>), and the --fixture <id> CLI argument.

description.description: States the expected behaviour, not just the input shape. e.g. 'Critical priority ticket — escalates to L2 immediately' (good); 'Ticket text with priority field' (bad).

inputs.description: Keys MUST match the run_pipeline parameter names exactly. Values are JSON scalars, lists, or nested objects; the writer renders them as Python literals via repr() with multi-line strings triple-quoted.
